text:
Market today: No major macro catalyst in today's headlines for tech/chips. The mood is cautiously sideways — oil easing and gold stalling suggest risk appetite isn't panicking, but there's no fresh fuel for your kind of names either. Watch for rotation risk as Wall Street chases other themes.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR is down 12.77% and flagged SELL — on margin, that hole gets expensive fast; no news to rescue it today. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45%, flagged TRIM — size plus loss is a dangerous combo on margin. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with barely any price history; even a small move can be violent, and margin amplifies that instantly. SPAL adds 2x leveraged decay on top — both SpaceX plays need tight manual oversight.

Watch: 1) Whether chip/semi sentiment shifts on the SK Hynix U.S. listing news — could pressure Micron and ripple into AVGO/SMH. 2) PLTR holding above its $85 stop.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are also your two biggest risks on margin — act on PLTR and AVGO before the market acts for you.
